revision.text:
[[ja:御廟山古墳]][[zh:御廟山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |name = Gobyō-yama Kofun |image = File:Mozu Gobyo-yama Kofun zenkei.JPG |alternate_name = Mozu Gobyō-yama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1042504|1=Kita-ku, Sakai}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.5547|N|135.4908|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = [[Zenpokoen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) |area = Total length: 203 m |burial = Unknown |built = Early 5th century |Occupant = (Tentative by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) Emperor Ōjin (15th Emperor of Japan) |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} and others |Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mozu Candidate Imperial Mausoleum" |notes = Ranked 35th in Japan by size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun by area (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Official Website, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Gobyō-yama Kofun |type = Zenpokoen-fun-NWN |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Gobyouyama Kofun Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|220px|Aerial view of Gobyō-yama Kofun (2007)
{{国土航空写真}}]] '''Gobyō-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|御廟山古墳}}) is a [[kofun]] (tumulus) located in Mozu Honmachi, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli typeing the [[Mozu Tombs|Mozu Kofun Group]] and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped) design. The inner moat of the site is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site of Japan as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group." While the actual Interred_individual of the burial is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the "Mozu Candidate Imperial Mausoleum," tentatively attributed to Emperor Ōjin (the 15th Emperor of Japan). == Overview == [[File:光明院 長持形石棺片.JPG|thumb|150px|right|{{center|Fragment of a {{ill|Nagamochigata Sekkan|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochigata Sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} at Kōmyōin Temple}}{{small|On display at Sakai City Museum. Believed to have been used in Gobyō-yama Kofun.}}]] The tumulus is located almost at the center of the [[Mozu Tombs|Mozu Kofun Group]], southeast of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]]. With a total length of about 203 meters, it is the fourth largest tumulus in the Mozu Kofun Group{{Sfn|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014|11=43-44}}. The mound is constructed in three tiers and includes a protruding section on the southern side. Excavations have confirmed the existence of a double moat that once surrounded the mound. Several accompanying mounds were also identified, though only the Mandai-yama Kofun remains{{Sfn|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014|11=43-44}}. Details of the burial structure and grave goods remain unclear due to limited archaeological investigation. The Imperial Household Agency currently manages the site as an "Candidate Imperial Mausoleum." In 2008, restoration work was carried out to prevent erosion and remove fallen trees. During this period, the mound's total length was remeasured and confirmed to be approximately 203 meters, revising the previously estimated length of 186 meters. It was also discovered that parts of the mound had been trimmed during the Edo period when the moats were expanded for agricultural water storage. In 2018, the inner moat was Historic Site1 as part of the National Historic Site "Mozu Kofun Group"Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 193, October 15, 2018. == Interred_individual == One theory suggests that the tumulus may belong to {{ill|Wa Sui|ja|倭隋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wa Sui|qq=}}, one of the prominent generals listed in the "Song Shu (History of the Song)" among the retainers who accompanied {{ill|Chin of Wa|en|Chin of Wa|qid=Q28692298|lt=Chin of Wa|1=Chin of Wa}} in receiving the title of "General." Wazu's inclusion in the record alongside Chin and his high Rank suggest he was a significant royal figureSeike, 2018, 11. 225–226Sōgō, 2012. == Accompanying Mound == * '''Mandai-yama Kofun''': Diameter 25 meters, height 4.3 meters. Although circular today, it may have originally been a "scallop-shaped tumulus." == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014|11=43-44}}: - Total length: 203 meters - Posterior section diameter: 113 meters - Anterior section width: 136 meters == Access == - 5-minute walk from [[JR West Japan|JR]] {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|qid=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}} {{ill|Mozu Station|en|Mozu Station|qid=Q856909|lt=Mozu Station|1=Mozu Station}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title=堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=百舌鳥陵墓参考地　墳丘裾護岸その他整備工事に伴う事前調査|title={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo061b032.pdf 書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号]}}|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010}}}} - Imperial Household Agency's archives. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/gobyoyama.html Gobyō-yama Kofun - Sakai City Official Website] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} }} {{translated page|ja|御廟山古墳|version=97231680|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:御廟山古墳]] [[zh:御廟山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11489009}} [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
